functions.name: addPythonApp
functions.description: Adds a Python application to the application model.
functions.signature: addPythonApp(name: string, appDirectory: string, scriptPath: string): PythonAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the python application.

parameters.name: scriptPath
parameters.description: The path to the script relative to the app directory to run.

functions.name: addPythonExecutable
functions.description: Adds a Python executable to the application model.
functions.signature: addPythonExecutable(name: string, appDirectory: string, executableName: string): PythonAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the python application.

parameters.name: executableName
parameters.description: The name of the executable in the virtual environment (e.g., "pytest", "uvicorn", "flask").

functions.name: addPythonModule
functions.description: Adds a Python module to the application model.
functions.signature: addPythonModule(name: string, appDirectory: string, moduleName: string): PythonAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The path to the directory containing the python application.

parameters.name: moduleName
parameters.description: The name of the Python module to run (e.g., "flask", "uvicorn").

functions.name: addUvicornApp
functions.description: Adds a Uvicorn-based Python application to the distributed application builder with HTTP endpoint configuration.
functions.remarks:
This method configures the application to use Uvicorn as the ASGI server and exposes an HTTP
endpoint. When publishing, it sets the entry point to use the Uvicorn executable with appropriate arguments for
host and port.
By default, the virtual environment folder is expected to be named `.venv` and located in the app directory.
Use `WithVirtualEnvironment``1` to specify a different virtual environment path.
In non-publish mode, the `--reload` flag is automatically added to enable hot reload during development.
functions.returns: A resource builder for further configuration of the Uvicorn Python application resource.
functions.signature: addUvicornApp(name: string, appDirectory: string, app: string): UvicornAppResource

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The unique name of the Uvicorn application resource.

parameters.name: appDirectory
parameters.description: The directory containing the Python application files.

parameters.name: app
parameters.description: The ASGI app import path which informs Uvicorn which module and variable to load as your web application. For example, "main:app" means "main.py" file and variable named "app".

functions.name: withDebugging
functions.description: Enables debugging support for the Python application.
functions.remarks:
This method adds the `PythonExecutableDebuggableAnnotation` to the resource, which enables
debugging support. The debugging configuration is automatically set up based on the
entrypoint type (Script, Module, or Executable).
The debug configuration includes the Python interpreter path from the virtual environment,
the program or module to debug, and appropriate launch settings.

functions.name: withEntrypoint
functions.description: Configures the entrypoint for the Python application.
functions.remarks:
This method allows you to change the entrypoint configuration of a Python application after it has been created.
The command and arguments will be updated based on the specified entrypoint type:
- Script: Runs as `python <scriptPath>`
- Module: Runs as `python -m <moduleName>`
- Executable: Runs the executable directly from the virtual environment
Important: This method resets all command-line arguments. If you need to add arguments after changing
the entrypoint, call `WithArgs` after this method.

parameters.name: entrypointType
parameters.description: The type of entrypoint (Script, Module, or Executable).

parameters.name: entrypoint
parameters.description: The entrypoint value (script path, module name, or executable name).

functions.name: withPip
functions.description: Configures the Python resource to use pip as the package manager and optionally installs packages before the application starts.
functions.remarks:
This method creates a child resource that runs `pip install` in the working directory of the Python application.
The Python application will wait for this resource to complete successfully before starting.
Pip will automatically detect and use either pyproject.toml or requirements.txt based on which file exists in the application directory.
If pyproject.toml exists, pip will use it. Otherwise, if requirements.txt exists, pip will use that.
Calling this method will replace any previously configured package manager (such as uv).

parameters.name: install
parameters.description: When true (default), automatically installs packages before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

parameters.name: installArgs
parameters.description: The command-line arguments passed to pip install command.

functions.name: withUv
functions.description: Adds a UV environment setup task to ensure the virtual environment exists before running the Python application.
functions.remarks:
This method creates a child resource that runs `uv sync` in the working directory of the Python application.
The Python application will wait for this resource to complete successfully before starting.
UV (https://github.com/astral-sh/uv) is a modern Python package manager written in Rust that can manage virtual environments
and dependencies with significantly faster performance than traditional tools. The `uv sync` command ensures that the virtual
environment exists, Python is installed if needed, and all dependencies specified in pyproject.toml are installed and synchronized.
Calling this method will replace any previously configured package manager (such as pip).

parameters.name: install
parameters.description: When true (default), automatically runs uv sync before the application starts. When false, only sets the package manager annotation without creating an installer resource.

parameters.name: args
parameters.description: Optional custom arguments to pass to the uv command. If not provided, defaults to ["sync"].

functions.name: withVirtualEnvironment
functions.description: Configures a custom virtual environment path for the Python application.
functions.remarks:
This method updates the Python executable path to use the specified virtual environment.
By default (`createIfNotExists` = `true`), if the virtual environment doesn't exist,
it will be automatically created before running the application (when using pip package manager, not uv).
Set `createIfNotExists` to `false` to disable this behavior and require the venv to already exist.
Virtual environments allow Python applications to have isolated dependencies separate from
the system Python installation. This is the recommended approach for Python applications.
When you explicitly specify a virtual environment path using this method, the path is used verbatim.
The automatic multi-location lookup (checking both app and AppHost directories) only applies when
using the default ".venv" path during initial app creation via AddPythonScript, AddPythonModule, or AddPythonExecutable.

parameters.name: virtualEnvironmentPath
parameters.description: The path to the virtual environment. Can be absolute or relative to the app directory. When relative, it is resolved from the working directory of the Python application. Common values include ".venv", "venv", or "myenv".

parameters.name: createIfNotExists
parameters.description: Whether to automatically create the virtual environment if it doesn't exist. Defaults to `true`. Set to `false` to disable automatic venv creation (the venv must already exist).

capabilities.name: withDebugging
capabilities.description: Enables debugging support for the Python application.
capabilities.remarks:
This method adds the `PythonExecutableDebuggableAnnotation` to the resource, which enables
debugging support. The debugging configuration is automatically set up based on the
entrypoint type (Script, Module, or Executable).
The debug configuration includes the Python interpreter path from the virtual environment,
the program or module to debug, and appropriate launch settings.

capabilities.description: Configures the entrypoint for the Python application.
capabilities.remarks:
This method allows you to change the entrypoint configuration of a Python application after it has been created.
The command and arguments will be updated based on the specified entrypoint type:
- Script: Runs as `python <scriptPath>`
- Module: Runs as `python -m <moduleName>`
- Executable: Runs the executable directly from the virtual environment
Important: This method resets all command-line arguments. If you need to add arguments after changing
the entrypoint, call `WithArgs` after this method.

parameters.name: entrypointType
parameters.description: The type of entrypoint (Script, Module, or Executable).

parameters.name: entrypoint
parameters.description: The entrypoint value (script path, module name, or executable name).

enumTypes.name: EntrypointType
enumTypes.description: Specifies the type of entrypoint for a Python application.

memberDocs.name: Executable
memberDocs.description: A standalone executable from the virtual environment (e.g., "uvicorn", "flask", "pytest").

memberDocs.name: Script
memberDocs.description: A Python script file to execute directly (e.g., "main.py", "app.py").

memberDocs.name: Module
memberDocs.description: A Python module to run via `python -m <module>` (e.g., "flask", "uvicorn").
